sourceStatus.publicationPolicy: No long SEC source text is embedded; evidence uses short digests and source URL anchors.

summaryKo.oneLine: 시스코 시스템즈는 2025 회계연도에 566억 5,400만 달러의 매출과 101억 8,000만 달러의 순이익을 기록하며 AI, 클라우드, 사이버 보안 분야에 대한 전략적 투자를 지속하고 있습니다.

keyPoints.text: AI, 클라우드, 사이버 보안 등 핵심 우선순위 분야에 자원을 집중하고 엔지니어링 및 영업 기능을 강화하는 장기 투자 전략을 추진 중입니다.
통신 및 네트워킹 시장 내 가격 경쟁과 제품 경쟁이 심화되고 있으며, 진입 장벽이 낮아 새로운 경쟁자의 시장 진입이 빈번합니다.
구조조정 활동 및 관련 비용 발생이 회사의 운영 결과와 재무 상태에 중대한 영향을 미칠 수 있음을 명시했습니다.

riskChanges.text: 글로벌 경제 환경의 불확실성으로 인해 서비스 제공업체 및 인터넷 비즈니스 부문의 제품 수요 변동성이 커지고 있습니다.
특허 및 지적 재산권 보호를 위해 노력하고 있으나, 기술 표준화와 경쟁사의 도전으로 인해 독점적 권리 확보가 어려울 수 있습니다.

businessChanges.text: 제품 판매 및 구현 주기의 변화로 인해 고객의 지출 계획에 대한 가시성이 감소하고 있으며, 이는 매출 변동성을 유발합니다.
핵심 네트워크 리더십을 유지하는 동시에 AI, 클라우드, 사이버 보안 분야로 자원을 재배치하는 전략적 리얼라이닝을 진행 중입니다.

financialHighlights.text: 2025 회계연도 매출은 566억 5,400만 달러, 영업이익은 117억 6,000만 달러를 기록했습니다.
순이익은 101억 8,000만 달러이며, 총자산은 1,222억 9,100만 달러, 현금및현금성자산은 83억 4,600만 달러를 보유하고 있습니다.

watchItems.text: 구조조정 및 신규 사업(AI, 보안) 투자 비용이 단기 수익성에 미치는 영향과, 경쟁 심화 속에서 핵심 네트워크 부문의 매출 방어 능력을 주시해야 합니다.

evidence.sourceTextDigest: FY2025 revenue = 566억 5,400만 달러 (USD 56.654B; 56,654 million), period ended 2025-07-26.
FY2025 operating income = 117억 6,000만 달러 (USD 11.76B; 11,760 million), period ended 2025-07-26.
FY2025 net income = 101억 8,000만 달러 (USD 10.18B; 10,180 million), period ended 2025-07-26.
FY2025 total assets = 1,222억 9,100만 달러 (USD 122.291B; 122,291 million), period ended 2025-07-26.
FY2025 cash and cash equivalents = 83억 4,600만 달러 (USD 8.346B; 8,346 million), period ended 2025-07-26.

Risk Factors Set forth below and elsewhere in this report and in other documents we file with the SEC are descriptions of the risks and uncertainties that could cause our actual results to differ materially from the results contemplated by the forward-looking statements contained in this report. Risks Related to our Business and Industry Our operations can be difficult to predict because our operating results may fluctuate in future periods. Our operating results have been in the past, and will continue to be, subject to quarterly and annual fluctuations as a result of numerous factors, some of which may contribute to more pronounced fluctuations in an uncertain global economic environment. These factors include: • Fluctuations in demand for our products and services, especially with respect to service providers and Internet businesses, in part due to changes i

Risks Related to our Business and Industry Our operations can be difficult to predict because our operating results may fluctuate in future periods. Our operating results have been in the past, and will continue to be, subject to quarterly and annual fluctuations as a result of numerous factors, some of which may contribute to more pronounced fluctuations in an uncertain global economic environment. These factors include: • Fluctuations in demand for our products and services, especially with respect to service providers and Internet businesses, in part due to changes in the global economic environment • Changes in sales and implementation cycles for our products and reduced visibility into our customers’ spending plans and associated revenue • Our ability to maintain appropriate inventory levels and purchase commitments • Price and product competition in the communications and networkin

Competition” contained in “Item 1. Business” of this report. Some of our competitors compete across many of our product lines, while others are primarily focused in a specific product area. Barriers to entry are relatively low, and new ventures to create products that do or could compete with our products are regularly formed. In addition, some of our competitors may have greater resources, including technical and engineering resources, than we do. As we expand into new markets, we will face competition not only from our existing competitors but also from other competitors, including existing companies with strong technological, marketing, and sales positions in those markets. We also sometimes face competition from resellers and distributors of our products. Companies with which we have strategic alliances in some areas may be competitors in other areas, and this trend may increase. For

Our business may not be more efficient or effective than prior to implementation of such plans. Our restructuring activities, including any related charges and the impact of the related headcount restructurings, could materially harm our business, operating results, and financial condition. Over the long term we intend to invest in engineering, sales, service and marketing activities, and in key priority areas, and these investments may achieve delayed, or lower than expected, benefits which could harm our operating results. While we intend to focus on managing our costs and expenses, over the long term, we also intend to invest in personnel and other resources related to our engineering, sales, service and marketing functions as we realign and dedicate resources on key priority areas, such as AI, cloud, and cybersecurity. We also intend to focus on maintaining leadership in core network

Intellectual Property Our proprietary rights may prove difficult to enforce. We generally rely on patents, copyrights, trademarks, and trade secret laws to establish and maintain proprietary rights in our technology and products. Although we have been issued numerous patents and other patent applications are currently pending, there can be no assurance that any of these patents or other proprietary rights will not be challenged, invalidated, or 22 Table of Contents circumvented or that our rights will, in fact, provide competitive advantages to us. Furthermore, many key aspects of our technology are governed by industry-wide standards, which are usable by all market entrants. In addition, there can be no assurance that patents will be issued from pending applications or that claims allowed on any patents will be sufficiently broad to protect our technology.
